content.problemStatement:
**This is the odd version of the Grid Construction Problem. Both are worth 50 points each.**

You are given an **odd** integer NNN.

Now your task is to construct a grid AAA containing NNN rows and NNN columns such that :


For every i,j(1≤i,j≤N)i,j(1 \le i,j \le N)i,j(1≤i,j≤N) 1≤Ai,j≤N1 \le A_{i,j} \le N1≤Ai,j​≤N have to be satisfied.
For every x(1≤x≤N)x(1 \le x \le N)x(1≤x≤N), xxx appears **exactly** NNN times in AAA.
Let S1S_1S1​ be the sum of elements in the diagonal from top-left to bottom-right and S2S_2S2​ be the sum of elements in the diagonal from top-right to bottom-left, Then :

Sum of all elements in every 222 adjacent rows has to be equal to S1+S2S_1 + S_2S1​+S2​.
Sum of all elements in every 222 adjacent columns has to be equal to S1+S2S_1 + S_2S1​+S2​.


It can be proven that at least one valid answer exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of input contains NNN - the dimension of the grid.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines each containing NNN integers. The jjj-th integer in the iii-th line should represent Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​ of the grid you constructed.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 500500500.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Here, S1=6,S2=6S_1 = 6, S_2 = 6S1​=6,S2​=6, and each row, column adds to 666, implying adjacent row, column adds to 12=S1+S212 = S_1 + S_212=S1​+S2​. Thus, it is valid.
